title: [lichen_l2.c:1038] lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort called from cross-module
description: Function: lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort. Contract violation: The function documents 'This function is NOT thread-safe. It must only be called from lora_l2_deinit()' but the header (lichen_l2.h:152) exports it publicly. Any external caller could invoke it at the wrong time, corrupting the mutex. Consider making it static and exposing via a function pointer set during init, or adding a guard (e.g., checking if the caller is holding a deinit-in-progress flag). Priority 2 because misuse leads to undefined behavior in a security-critical path.
close_reason: Fixed: Added k_panic() precondition check that refuses to reinit mutex if module is running, plus strengthened SECURITY comments in both .c and .h files documenting the danger and correct usage
